evidence.scientific_clarity: All brief requirements met: violin+box overlay, jittered points (alpha=0.35≤0.4), white-diamond medians, correct Control→low→high→Combo ordering, and optional significance brackets included.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype='none' set anywhere in the script; font family defaults to DejaVu Sans rather than a publication-quality choice.
evidence.palette: PALETTE contains #55A868 (green) and #C44E52 (red) as adjacent categorical conditions — indistinguishable for deuteranopes/protanopes; not a colorblind-safe set.
evidence.layout_density: tight_layout() used, two-line x-tick labels avoid overlap, bracket spacing is dynamic; legend in upper-right partially duplicates x-axis labels, adding minor clutter.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes all visual elements and the statistical test; legend is redundant with x-axis labels; missing fonttype=42 will draw a technical comment from a careful reviewer.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf is saved but rcParams['pdf.fonttype'] is never set to 42, so text is embedded as Type 3 (bitmap) fonts rather than selectable/editable outlines.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all confirmed present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains every visual element (KDE violins, IQR boxes, 1.5×IQR whiskers, diamond medians, jittered points, significance brackets, reference line) and names the test, but states no specific numerical finding (e.g., median viability of Combo vs Control).

overall_strengths: Complete output set (PDF, PNG, SVG, caption, script) with all brief-required visual layers present and correctly ordered.
Caption is thorough and self-consistent with the figure, covering every graphical element and the statistical methodology.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' means text in both PDF and SVG is non-selectable Type 3 bitmap — a standard journal submission requirement that is unmet.
Red (#C44E52) and green (#55A868) assigned to adjacent categorical conditions make the figure inaccessible to red-green colorblind readers (~8% of males).
